record_identity.title: Repo access inferred before explicit permission confirmation

summary: Observed runtime behaviour in which an assistant appeared to infer permission to access a repository or tool context after the user mentioned repo-level work, initiated or attempted tool access before explicit user confirmation, and caused the conversation stream or workflow continuity to become blocked or unrecoverable until the user edited the turn or explicitly prohibited tool access.
review_observation: A subsequent repo-linked diagnostic request did not reproduce the streaming freeze; tool invocation completed successfully. The pattern should remain under observation pending recurrence testing.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This observation may matter to CAM because tool invocation is not only a capability issue but also a continuity, consent, and permission-boundary issue. Where external access requires user-interface confirmation, premature tool execution may degrade user agency, interrupt response continuity, and force the user to perform corrective work to restore the interaction. CAM runtime governance may need to distinguish contextual discussion of a repository, file, connector, or external system from explicit authorization to access it.

source_records.source_title: ChatGPT interaction observation — repo access inferred before permission confirmation
source_records.source_context: Observed ChatGPT interaction in which repo-level work was referenced, assistant behaviour appeared to infer tool or repository access before explicit permission confirmation, and unresolved permission gating caused a streaming or continuity failure requiring user correction.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary observed interaction pattern: user referenced repo-level work, assistant appeared to infer tool access, and unresolved permission gating caused a streaming or continuity failure requiring user correction.

source_data.reproducibility: not reproduced in subsequent diagnostic request

cam_internal.routing_note: Observation preserved as a watching-state source record. It may inform FM-0001 or a future proposal, but this OBS record does not itself classify the failure, propose repair logic, or claim implementation.

next_action: Watch for recurrence or corroborating examples. If recurrence is observed, compare against VIGIL-2026-FM-0001 or create/update a related failure mode record. Do not create a proposal or patch note unless a concrete CAM repair path is separately drafted or implemented.
